title: plesioneuron tuberculatum
side_effects: Plesioneuron tuberculatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems. Ingesting the plant's leaves or stems can cause nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, while prolonged exposure may lead to liver and kidney damage. Improper use of the plant's extracts may also cause dizziness, headaches, and changes in heart rate and blood pressure. In severe cases, using Plesioneuron tuberculatum without proper guidance can result in seizures, coma, or even death.
